record.field: bolt.diy is the open-source, self-hostable AI app builder that lets you prompt, run, edit, and deploy full-stack web applications using any LLM you choose.

bolt.diy is the open-source, self-hostable AI app builder that lets you prompt, run, edit, and deploy full-stack web applications using any LLM you choose.

It is the official community fork of bolt.new, pairing a conversational coding agent with an in-browser WebContainer sandbox that runs a full Node.js environment, terminal, and live preview. Unlike hosted alternatives, it is provider-agnostic: you connect your own model from cloud APIs or local engines, so it suits developers and teams who want full control over their stack and their AI costs.

Key features
  • Generate, run, and edit full-stack apps from natural-language prompts, with an integrated terminal and live preview
  • Bring-your-own-LLM across 19+ providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Mistral, DeepSeek, Groq and more) plus local engines like Ollama and LM Studio
  • Isolated WebContainer sandbox with file-system access, diff viewing, file locking, and Git integration
  • Model Context Protocol (MCP) support, Supabase database integration, and project templates for popular frameworks
  • One-click deploys to Netlify, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, and GitHub Pages, plus image-attachment context
  • Flexible delivery: Docker images for self-hosting, a desktop Electron app, or direct Node.js install
Best for

Developers, agencies, and product teams who want an AI-assisted, prompt-to-app workflow they can self-host and steer with their own choice of language model. Ideal for rapid prototyping, internal tools, and full-stack web app scaffolding without depending on a single proprietary AI vendor. Note the WebContainers API requires a commercial license for for-profit production use.
